text.title: Incident Response Blameless Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Incident Response Blameless Playbook application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Sev1-Sev5 matrix with explicit triggers (revenue impact, user count, data integrity, regulator clock). Sev1 = pages CEO/CTO; Sev5 = next business day.
rule.rationale: Without a matrix, every issue is 'critical' and team burns out; matrix is the only sustainable triage.

rule.statement: Sev1 + Sev2 incidents have a named Incident Commander who is NOT debugging — they coordinate. Engineers debug; IC drives.
rule.rationale: Single-engineer-doing-everything fails at >30-min incidents; IC role is the operational backbone.

rule.statement: Two comms channels: internal (Slack #incidents, every 15-30 min update) and external (status page + customer email for impact ≥Sev3). Both have templates.
rule.rationale: Silence creates panic + speculation; templates remove drafting overhead during stress.

rule.statement: Every Sev1-Sev3 has a blameless retro within 5 business days. Retro names systems + decisions, not people. ≥1 action item with owner + deadline.
rule.rationale: Westrum, A Typology of Organisational Cultures: blameless culture is the leading indicator of high-performing teams; blame culture predicts attrition.

rule.statement: Action items from retros are tracked in the same backlog as features. Stale action items >30 days trigger escalation.
rule.rationale: Retros without follow-through are theatre; the action queue is the only thing that compounds.
